concerns.text: FR-004 mandates generating '≥ 200 hypothetical ABX₃ compositions' and filtering for '< -0.1 eV/atom'. However, User Story 3 (US-3) explicitly requires a 'combinatorial library of [deferred] hypothetical ABX₃ formulas' and an output of 'top 20 candidates'. The FR's specific count (200) and filtering logic (all < -0.1) are not anchored to any story's scope. US-3 only anchors to the 'top 20' output. This creates a potential scope creep where the system must generate 200+ items but the story only validates the top 20. The FR should be re-anchored to US-3 with a note that the 200 count is an internal generation target, or the story should be updated to reflect the 200-generation requirement.

SC-003 states: 'The virtual screening output is measured against the requirement to produce a ranked list of ≥ 200 hypothetical compositions, with the top 10 flagged for experimental follow-up'. This contradicts User Story 3 (US-3), which specifies 'top 20 candidates' in its acceptance scenarios. SC-003 is anchored to US-3 and FR-004, but the specific metric (top 10 vs top 20) is inconsistent with the story it claims to serve. This is a writing error in the success criteria definition relative to the anchored story.
